channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Fatal Crash in Richmond Hill Leaves One Dead, Three Injured
item.description: A tragic accident occurred early this morning in Richmond Hill, Ontario, when a single vehicle crashed, killing one person and leaving three others in critical condition. Police have confirmed that th...

item.title: Sabastian Sawe’s Prize Money and Bonuses After London Marathon Record Performance
item.description: Kenyan athlete Sabastian Sawe reportedly delivered a historic performance at the 2026 London Marathon, clocking 1:59:30 to win the race and allegedly becoming the first person to run a marathon in und...

item.title: Zamfara Ex-Commissioner Shot by Bandits While Travelling
item.description: Omo, things dey really tough for Zamfara State! Former Commissioner for Religious Affairs, Abdullahi Maharazu Mafara, almost lost his life on Saturday after bandits attacked him while he was on his wa...

item.title: Recto refutes claim he hindered proposed minimum wage increase in Congress
item.description: Finance Secretary Ralph Recto has denied allegations made by Batangas Representative Leandro Leviste that he blocked efforts to pass a minimum wage increase during the 19th Congress. The issue highlig...

item.title: Buckingham Palace reviews security arrangements for King Charles III's upcoming US visit after White House Correspondents' Dinner incident
item.description: King Charles III is being kept fully informed following a shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner in Washington DC, Buckingham Palace has stated. The armed man breached a checkpoin...

item.title: Goa Police take YouTuber Gautam Khattar on remand over remarks on Saint Francis Xavier
item.description: On Sunday, YouTuber Gautam Khattar was brought to Goa on transit remand following his arrest in Himachal Pradesh, related to his alleged offensive remarks against St Francis Xavier, who is venerated a...

item.title: Alcaraz ruled out of French Open with wrist injury as Sinner eyes career Grand Slam
item.description: Carlos Alcaraz has been ruled out of the upcoming French Open after a wrist injury forced him to withdraw from the clay-court season, dealing a major blow to both his campaign and the tournament itsel...

item.title: TikTok formalises its influence on reading with new UK BookTok bestseller chart
item.description: TikTok’s growing influence on reading habits has been formalised with the launch of the UK’s first official BookTok bestseller list. Compiled using both UK sales figures and data from the #BookTok has...

item.title: Extreme Weather Events Linked to Increased Risk of Heart Disease
item.description: A recent study highlights the growing connection between extreme weather events, such as heatwaves, severe cold, and heavy rainfall, and an increased risk of heart disease, particularly in middle-aged...

item.title: Overview of Threats Against Trump Since White House Correspondents' Dinner Shooting
item.description: Over the years following the shooting incident at the White House Correspondents' Dinner, former President Donald Trump has been the target of multiple threats to his safety. These threats have varied...

item.title: Two San Diego Men Sentenced for Smuggling Endangered Birds Across the U.S. Border
item.description: Two men from San Diego were sentenced for smuggling endangered exotic birds into the United States, reflecting an ongoing wildlife trafficking problem at the U.S.-Mexico border. Ricardo Alonzo receive...

item.title: Increasing Threats to Indonesian Activists Evident Through Acid Attacks
item.description: Recent incidents in Indonesia show a troubling rise in attacks against activists, particularly through the use of acid. These attacks have raised concerns among civil society groups and human rights o...

item.title: How Mentorship and Support Helped a Student Overcome Dyslexia
item.description: Felicity Wadsworth, a 21-year-old student, faced numerous challenges throughout her school years due to dyslexia. However, her journey took a positive turn after she enrolled in a weekly after-school ...

item.title: Durban artist Anusha Singh builds a niche with bespoke hand-painted saris
item.description: Durban-based artist Anusha Singh, 56, has developed a distinctive creative business by transforming traditional saris into hand-painted works of wearable art. Operating from her brand, Studio Anusha, ...

item.title: Jigawa SEMA Issues Flood Warning, Lists Vulnerable Areas, Urges Preparedness
item.description: The Jigawa State Emergency Management Agency (SEMA) has issued a flood warning for several parts of the state, urging residents to take preventive measures as the rainy season approaches. In a stateme...

item.title: Fatal Single-Vehicle Crash in East Gwillimbury Leaves One Dead and Three Critically Injured
item.description: A tragic accident occurred in East Gwillimbury, Ontario, on April 26, 2026, when a single-vehicle crash resulted in one fatality and three individuals suffering critical injuries. Emergency services r...

item.title: Matty Juniosa secures spot in 'Britain's Got Talent' finals with another Golden Buzzer
item.description: Filipino singer Matty Juniosa has once again captured the attention of audiences and judges on the international stage. Following a recent performance on 'Britain's Got Talent,' Matty earned another G...

item.title: Sebastian Sawe Sets First Official Sub-Two-Hour Marathon Record at London Marathon
item.description: Kenyan runner Sebastian Sawe made history at the London Marathon by becoming the first athlete to complete a marathon in under two hours during an official competitive race. Sawe finished with a time ...

item.title: Taylor backs preferencing One Nation over Farrer independent in May byelection
item.description: Opposition leader Angus Taylor has justified putting One Nation ahead of high-profile independent Michelle Milthorpe in the upcoming Farrer byelection, describing it as the 'least worst option'. With ...
